long_title_contained_evidence:
apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx:257 h2 class changed from `font-semibold` to `min-w-0 break-words font-semibold` inside an existing flex-wrap row (`flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2`, line 256) nested in an already-min-w-0 wrapper (line 255). The h2's text comes from summarize() (components/approvals/lib.ts:112-127) which embeds unbounded details.jobTitle/company — the correct target field. apps/web/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx:596-601: dashboard 'Needs Approval' widget subtitle (job_title/company join, unbounded) gets `min-w-0 break-words` on the

plus `min-w-0` on the parent

  • (line 593) inside a `flex flex-col`
      (line 588). The fixed h2/subtitle are the right elements: the widget's own title text (approvalTitle(), page.tsx:72-78) is a fixed literal ('Send email' / 'Submit application' / etc.), never unbounded, correctly left untouched. Verified with a 300-char unbroken string in both new tests (page.test.tsx, dashboard/__tests__/page.test.tsx) — both fail against pre-fix code, pass against the fix.
back_history_sound_evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx: openReview (line ~126) pushState({approvalReview:id}), popstate listener (line ~110-120) closes the modal only when ?review is absent from the post-pop URL (reads window.location fresh, no stale closure), closeReview (line ~132) consumes the pushed entry via history.back() when ?review is present else falls back to replaceState. Deep-link effect (line ~85-103) splices a bare (no-review) entry via replaceState under a freshly pushed review entry, guaranteeing Back always lands on a same-page, no-review entry instead of leaving /dashboard/approvals. Traced two decide flows (decideFromCard/decideFromModal): approve/reject on an already-resolved approval is blocked by the backend state machine (approval_service.py:46-64, terminal pending->approved|rejected) and by the UI itself (approve/reject buttons only render for status==='pending', modal disables them when !pending) so a genuine double-pop scenario cannot be reached through the wired UI paths. One low-probability edge case noted under concerns (rapid X-close immediately followed by re-open before the async popstate from history.back() resolves) — not realistically triggerable by human timing and not covered by a test, but does not corrupt final state in any traced scenario.
errors_independent_evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx: `error = deepLinkError ?? listError` (line ~65); load() only ever writes listError (setListError, lines 76/81), the deep-link effect only ever writes deepLinkError (line 101), openReview clears deepLinkError only (line ~128) on successful review-open. No code path writes to the other's slot. New test 'invalid deep-link error is never clobbered by the list load' explicitly races a slow fetchApprovals resolution against an already-set deep-link error and confirms the alert text survives — fails against pre-fix single-error-state code, passes against the fix.
confidence_clamped_evidence: apps/web/src/components/approvals/lib.ts:50-54 — added `if (value > 1 && !Number.isInteger(value)) return null;` before the existing scale/round logic. Verified by hand: 1.5 -> null (was '2%' pre-fix, a nonsense rounding of a value that's neither a valid [0,1] fraction nor a real percentage), 2.3 -> null, 2 -> 2 (integer >1, valid already-scaled %), 96 -> 96, 0.876 -> 88 (fraction path untouched, condition requires value>1 so it never triggers for fractions <=1). New lib.test.ts case fails pre-fix (asserted null, pre-fix returned 2), passes post-fix.
execute_only_email_send_no_double_send_e: apps/web/src/components/approvals/api.ts: new executeApproval(id) -> POST /approvals/{id}/execute. Backend (apps/api/app/routers/approvals.py:132-144, UNCHANGED by this diff) dispatches _execute_email_send only for type==='email_send'; other types get a no-op 'executed' status record — confirms the fixer's premise that execute is the real Gmail-send path and is otherwise inert, matching the code comment in api.ts. Frontend gating: sendIfEmailApproval() (page.tsx) only calls executeApproval when `decision === 'approve' && resolved.type === 'email_send'` — verified by a passing test ('does not call execute for a non-email_send approval type') that ALSO passes unmodified against pre-fix code (trivially true pre-fix, since nothing called execute before this diff at all) — this is the expected 1-of-9 pre-fix pass, not a defect in the test. No-double-send analysis: sendIfEmailApproval is invoked exactly once per successful decideApproval(.... 'approve') resolution in both decideFromCard and decideFromModal; the backend's approval state machine (approval_service.py: resolve()) only permits a pending->approved transition ONCE (409 on any subsequent attempt), and the UI itself removes/disables approve controls once status leaves 'pending' (card: renders approve/reject only if status==='pending'; modal: buttons disabled when !pending) — so no UI-reachable path can invoke sendIfEmailApproval twice for the same approval. apiRequest's only automatic retry (lib/api/client.ts:93-100) is a single retry on 401 (auth-expired) BEFORE any handler side effect runs, so it cannot cause a second real send. Honest error path confirmed: on executeApproval() rejection, sendIfEmailApproval catches and returns a user-facing string ('Approved, but sending the email failed: <reason>') via setListError — no fake/silent success, and the underlying backend send path (_execute_email_send, approvals.py:147-220) fails honestly with 409/422 (no Gmail connected, missing recipient, bad attachment) before ever calling GmailService.send. CONCERN (not a fail reason, flagged for follow-up): the backend's assert_action_allowed (approval_service.py:66-79) only checks status==='approved', with no executedAt/idempotency column — an out-of-band double POST /execute (buggy client, curl replay, or a slow-send-then-client-timeout retry) could still double-send. This gap is pre-existing and NOT introduced by this diff (approvals.py is untouched), but this diff is what makes the endpoint newly reachable via normal UI usage, so it should be logged as a new backend-hardening finding rather than block this frontend-scoped fix.
tests_meaningful_evidence: Ran the 9 new/changed assertions against the pre-fix production files (page.tsx, dashboard/page.tsx, lib.ts, api.ts checked out from 9e57c24^ while keeping the new *.test.tsx/*.test.ts at 9e57c24) inside the fixer's own worktree (/home/ubuntu/github_repos/aether-job-career-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-a06736d0b0db0cf44), then restored to 9e57c24 afterward (git checkout 9e57c24 -- <4 files>, working tree left clean). Result: 8 failed / 1 passed against pre-fix code, matching the claimed '8/9 fail-before' exactly. The 1 pre-fix pass ('does not call execute for a non-email_send approval type') is a legitimate non-tautology — it trivially holds pre-fix because execute was never called for anything yet, not because the assertion is vacuous. All 9 assert concrete, specific behavior (exact CSS classes, exact mock call args/ids, exact error text substrings, exact history/URL state) rather than existence-only checks.
vitest_result: 431 passed / 431 total (62 test files), 0 failed — run in worktree at commit 9e57c24, apps/web, `pnpm vitest run`, duration ~80s.
tsc_result: clean — `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` produced no output/errors.
build_result: `pnpm run build` (next build) succeeded: compiled, typechecked, and statically generated all 29 routes including /dashboard/approvals (6.54 kB) and /dashboard (8.04 kB) with no errors.
prohibited_patterns_notes: Searched the full commit diff for eslint-disable, @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error, Math.random, TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX, 'as any'/': any', --no-verify. One hit: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/__tests__/page.test.tsx:76 `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` — this is the standard, repo-wide vitest pattern (20 other existing test files use the identical directive) required because vi.hoisted mock declarations must precede the `import ApprovalsPage from "../page"` statement; it is test-only, does not suppress any production error handling, and is not a new pattern introduced by this fix. No placeholder/mock content reachable by real users, no suppressed errors in production code paths, no fake scores/success, no scope creep into files outside the stated cluster (backend approvals.py/approval_service.py were read for verification only, not touched by this diff).
reasons: All 6 findings' root causes are addressed at the correct code locations, not just symptomatically patched (verified line-by-line against both the fixer's diff and the actual pre-existing backend contract for MV-008).
Diff is minimal and scoped to the named files; no unrelated refactors or scope creep.

8/9 new tests independently verified to fail against pre-fix code and pass against the fix, via direct execution in the fixer's own worktree (not taken on the fixer's word); the 9th's pre-fix pass is legitimate, not tautological.
vitest 431/431, tsc clean, next build clean — all independently re-run in this review, not merely re-quoted from the fixer's report.

Execute wiring (MV-008) is correctly restricted to email_send + approve, cannot be double-fired through any UI-reachable path given the backend's single-transition approval state machine and the UI's own pending-gated controls, and surfaces an honest (non-silent) error on send failure.
No prohibited patterns found beyond a pre-existing, repo-wide, test-only eslint-disable convention.
concerns: Backend POST /approvals/{id}/execute (apps/api/app/routers/approvals.py:132-144, approval_service.py:66-79) has no executedAt/idempotency guard — only checks status==='approved'. Pre-existing gap, not introduced by this diff, but this diff makes the endpoint newly reachable via ordinary UI usage. Recommend filing a follow-up backend-hardening finding (add an executed/executedAt column and 409 on re-execute) rather than blocking this frontend fix on it.

No manual 'retry send' UI affordance exists if the auto-fired executeApproval fails after approve (ApprovalModal.tsx has no execute/resend button); the surfaced error text sometimes says 'please retry from the approval' with no action to do so. The error is honest (no fake success) but the recommended remediation is not actionable from the UI. Worth a small follow-up, not a blocker for this MED/LOW-severity cluster.

Forward navigation after a Back-triggered modal close does not reopen the modal (popstate handler only ever closes, never reopens/refetches) — outside the stated MV-approval-modal-005 scope (which was specifically about Back leaving the screen), but noted for completeness.

Theoretical (not realistically human-triggerable, untested) race: closeReview's programmatic history.back() and an immediate subsequent openReview() before the async popstate resolves could interleave; traced through but not covered by an automated test. Low risk, not blocking.
